name: DCVC (Data Collective)
company: Data Collective DCVC Management Company, LLC

orgInfo.description: Deep-tech venture capital firm led by Matt Ocko and Zachary Bogue, investing across AI, robotics, exa-scale computing, climate, materials science, space and biology. Led Agility Robotics' Series B alongside Playground Global.

sources.title: Agility Robotics Raises $150M Series B Led By DCVC and Playground Global

_provenance.notes: Lead investor of Agility Robotics Series B.

orgInfo.description: Deep-tech venture firm founded in 2015 by Andy Rubin, Bruce Leak, Matt Hershenson and Peter Barrett. Manages more than $1.2B AUM across three funds and is one of the most active Silicon Valley investors in robotics and next-generation compute.

sources.title: Playground is betting big on robots | TechCrunch

_provenance.notes: Co-led Agility Robotics Series B with DCVC.

orgInfo.description: Early-stage deep-tech VC led by Karthee Madasamy, investing across physical AI & robotics, quantum computing, semiconductors and climate & energy. Existing Series B investor in Agility Robotics.

sources.title: MFV Partners – Early Stage Deep-Tech VC in Silicon Valley

_provenance.notes: Cited as previous investor in Agility Series B.

orgInfo.description: Venture capital arm of Taiwan's Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI), founded in 1979. Has invested over US$400M globally and is an original shareholder of UMC, Taiwan Mask Corp and AU Optronics; previous investor in Agility Robotics.

_provenance.notes: ITIC = ITRI's investment subsidiary.

orgInfo.description: Pittsburgh-based early-stage robotics-focused venture fund that spun out of Carnegie Mellon's Robotics Ventures Initiative in late 2015 and is now operated as Coal Hill Ventures. Previous investor in Agility Robotics.

sources.title: Robotics Hub | Est. Coal Hill Ventures
The Robotics Hub investment portfolio | PitchBook

_provenance.notes: Distinct from the nonprofit Pittsburgh Robotics Network.

orgInfo.description: Boston-based seed-to-growth venture fund with deep ties to MIT, Harvard and the University of Rochester, founded in 2018 by Arunas Chesonis and Nader Motamedy. Invests across cleantech, AI/IT & robotics and life sciences with over $1B AUM.

sources.title: Safar Partners | Investing in Tomorrow's World-Changers.
New firm Safar targets early tech from Harvard, MIT and UR

_provenance.notes: Listed as previous Agility Robotics Series B investor.

orgInfo.description: Sony Group's corporate venture arm, established in July 2016, with offices in Japan, North America, Europe and India. Focuses on AI, robotics, digital health, IoT and entertainment; has made 60+ investments globally and participated in Agility Robotics' Series B.

sources.title: Why Sony Venture Fund is Still Bullish on Robots

_provenance.notes: Sony CVC arm.

orgInfo.description: Corporate venture arm of Japanese components maker TDK Corporation, founded in 2019 and based in San Jose. Manages roughly $500M across three funds investing in materials, energy, mobility, semiconductors and robotics; portfolio includes Agility Robotics and Groq.

sources.title: Investing in Tomorrow's Tech Today | TDK Ventures
TDK Ventures takes the wrapper off new $150m fund

name: Shanghai Longcheer Technology 龙旗科技

_provenance.notes: Industrial strategic investor (consumer-electronics ODM).

orgInfo.description: Shenzhen-listed (600580) Chinese motor and drive specialist with 40+ years of experience in frameless torque motors, reducers, servo drives and integrated joint modules. Strategic shareholder in AgiBot and operator of its own humanoid robotics arm SIR.

orgInfo.description: Zhuhai state-owned conglomerate established in 1980 with assets above RMB 650B across city operations, real estate, finance and industrial investment. Backed AgiBot and other embodied-AI startups via its industrial investment arm.

name: China Mobile 中国移动
company: China Mobile Communications Group Co., Ltd.

orgInfo.description: State-owned telecom giant and one of the world's largest mobile operators. Co-led Unitree's Series C in 2025 alongside Tencent, Alibaba, Ant Group, Geely Capital and Jinqiu, and has placed RMB 124M of robot orders with Unitree and AgiBot in 2025.

name: iFlytek Industry Fund 科大讯飞产业基金

orgInfo.description: Shanghai-headquartered boutique investment bank and asset manager founded in 2014, focused on new-economy advisory and direct investment. Manages over $400M AUM and has advised more than 50 unicorns and listed companies including Bilibili and Kuaishou.

orgInfo.description: Shanghai-based growth-stage private equity firm founded in 2012, focused on China's consumer megatrends (modernization, new retail, lifestyle, health & wellness, digital consumer). orgInfo.description: Venture firm founded in 2015 by Alibaba co-founder Wu Yongming, with an 'industry + investment' DNA. Manages assets of over RMB 15B and has invested in 150+ projects across hard tech, smart manufacturing, industrial digitalization, medical tech and cross-border expansion.

orgInfo.description: Lenovo Group's global technology industry fund established in 2016, operating a 'CVC 2.0' model that bundles Lenovo's resources with capital.
